title: supergateway-rs: Rust Rewrite using Asupersync
description:
Rewrite supergateway (~2,100 LOC TypeScript MCP transport bridge) in Rust using Asupersync.
Drop-in CLI replacement bridging MCP stdio servers with SSE, Streamable HTTP, and WebSocket transports.

6 transport modes: stdio→SSE, stdio→WS, stdio→Stateful HTTP, stdio→Stateless HTTP, SSE→stdio, HTTP→stdio.

Targets: <3MB RSS idle, <2ms p99 bridge latency, >10k msg/sec throughput, zero leaked children/FDs.

Source: /c/work/supergateway-rs/PRD.md (v6)

title: US-000: Asupersync Web Stack Validation Spike
description:
GO/NO-GO GATE — Validate Asupersync can serve the required workloads before committing to the rewrite.

This is a time-boxed spike (1-2 days) to build small proof-of-concept programs exercising every Asupersync capability the project needs. Each item is marked works / works-with-fallback / blocked. If any critical item is blocked with no fallback, the project does not proceed.

## Tech Stack
- Runtime: Asupersync 0.2.7 (NOT Tokio)
- Nightly Rust (pin in rust-toolchain.toml)
- All async operations use `&mut Cx` context

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] HTTP server: POST/GET/DELETE routes via `asupersync::web::Router` or `App::builder()`
- [ ] SSE streaming: push events one-at-a-time over long-lived connection. If batch-only, demonstrate raw chunked `text/event-stream`
- [ ] WebSocket: upgrade via `WebSocketUpgrade` extractor or manual HTTP upgrade + `net::websocket`. Bidirectional exchange
- [ ] HTTP client: GET and POST via `asupersync::http` or raw TCP
- [ ] SSE client: connect, receive streamed events
- [ ] Child process: determine if `asupersync::process::Command` exists. If not, demonstrate `std::process::Command` with async I/O adapter inside a Region
- [ ] Verify `kill_on_drop` or equivalent — child killed when Region closes
- [ ] Verify `&mut Cx` vs `&Cx` in handler signatures. **Critical:** Determine how concurrent HTTP request handlers each get their own `&mut Cx`. Does the framework fork/split contexts internally? This is a blocking architectural question
- [ ] Timer: `asupersync::time::timeout` wrapping a future
- [ ] Region lifecycle: spawn tasks, cancel region, verify cleanup
- [ ] Sync primitives: `mutex.lock(&cx).await?` works as documented
- [ ] Evaluate `fastmcp_rust` — reusable MCP handling? Document findings
- [ ] LabRuntime: deterministic test with virtual time works
- [ ] Nightly Rust: pin toolchain version, verify Docker build
- [ ] **Go/no-go documented.** For each item: works / works-with-fallback / blocked. If any critical item is blocked with no fallback, project does not proceed

## Quality Gates
- `cargo +nightly build --release` — no warnings
- `cargo +nightly test` — all tests pass
- `cargo +nightly clippy -- -D warnings` — no warnings
notes:
Deliverable: Create spike/FINDINGS.md with a table:
| Item | Status | Notes | Fallback Approach |
Status values: works / works-with-fallback / blocked

Each US-005+ bead references "per US-000 findings" for API availability decisions.
The findings file MUST be at a predictable path for downstream beads to consume.

title: US-009: Signal Handling and Graceful Shutdown
description:
Handle SIGINT, SIGTERM, SIGHUP, and stdin EOF for graceful shutdown. Includes PID 1 zombie reaping for container deployments.

## File: Signal handling in src/main.rs, PID 1 reaper as background task

## Signals Handled
- SIGINT, SIGTERM, SIGHUP → graceful shutdown
- stdin EOF/close → graceful shutdown (critical for client modes where parent may close stdin)

## Shutdown Sequence
1. Stop accepting new sessions/requests
2. Cancel server Region (triggers cascade: request→drain→finalize)
3. Each Region drop: send SIGTERM to process group (killpg)
4. Wait up to 5s for child exit
5. If still alive: SIGKILL to process group
6. Drain remaining stdout/stderr (up to 1MB, then truncate with log warning)
7. Close all FDs
8. Exit process

## Cancellation Points
- `cx.checkpoint()?` at all async yield points
- Region cancellation cascades to all spawned tasks

## PID 1 Behavior (Container)
- When running as PID 1 (Docker without tini/dumb-init): must reap zombie children
- Install SIGCHLD handler that calls `waitpid(-1, WNOHANG)` in a loop until ECHILD
- Run this as background task in server Region
- Document recommendation to use tini/dumb-init as entrypoint

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] SIGINT, SIGTERM, SIGHUP handled
- [ ] stdin EOF/close triggers graceful shutdown equivalent to SIGTERM
- [ ] Shutdown sequence: stop accepting → cancel server Region → cascade → exit
- [ ] `cx.checkpoint()?` at all cancellation points
- [ ] PID 1 behavior: properly reap zombie children when running as container init
- [ ] Integration test: start + SIGTERM → child pgid killed within 2s, gateway exits cleanly, zero zombies
- [ ] Integration test: stdin close → same behavior as SIGTERM
- [ ] Integration test: PID 1 + SIGTERM → children reaped, clean exit
- [ ] Verify: `/proc/<pid>/fd` count at 3 (stdin/stdout/stderr) after shutdown

## Quality Gates
- `cargo +nightly build --release` — no warnings
- `cargo +nightly test` — all tests pass
- `cargo +nightly clippy -- -D warnings` — no warnings

title: US-010: Gateway — stdio->Streamable HTTP (Stateful)
description:
Stateful Streamable HTTP gateway: per-session child processes, session management with Mcp-Session-Id, access counting, and full MCP SDK HTTP semantics.

## Tech Stack
- Asupersync web framework for HTTP routing
- Uses: ChildBridge (US-005), SessionManager (US-006), CORS (US-007), Logging (US-008), Codec (US-004)

## File: src/gateway/stdio_to_stateful_http.rs

## HTTP Endpoints
- **POST /mcp**: Forward JSON-RPC to child. First POST with `initialize` → spawn child in session Region, return Mcp-Session-Id header
- **GET /mcp**: SSE notification stream (one per session, 409 if already open). Content-Type: text/event-stream
- **DELETE /mcp**: Session termination (idempotent). Transition to Closing, drain in-flight, kill child

## Request Validation (Must reimplement from SDK)
1. Accept header: MUST include BOTH `application/json` AND `text/event-stream` → 406
2. Content-Type: MUST be `application/json` → 415
3. Session validation: wrong/missing Mcp-Session-Id → 404 (not 400 — matches SDK)
4. Protocol version: check against supported versions ["2025-06-18", "2025-03-26", "2024-11-05", "2024-10-07"]

## POST Response Types
- Notification-only POST (no `id` in body): HTTP 202 Accepted, empty body
- Request POST (has `id`): SSE stream response — Content-Type: text/event-stream, one `event: message\ndata: <json>\n\n` per response
- Stream closes when all requests in batch are answered
- Server-initiated notifications MAY be interleaved in SSE stream

## Session Lifecycle
- Mcp-Session-Id created on initialize, included in all subsequent responses
- Session counter: inc on request accept, dec on res.finish or res.close event
- Timeout fires when counter=0 (per US-006)
- Transport onclose/onerror: clear session counter (runCleanup=false), delete transport, kill child

## Body Parsing
- express.json() equivalent: parse JSON body before handler (unlike SSE mode which reads raw)

## Concurrency
- Serialized stdin writes to child via mutex
- Response correlation by JSON-RPC id
- Bounded channel per session (256 msgs) for backpressure

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] POST /mcp + no session + initialize → spawn child in session Region, return Mcp-Session-Id
- [ ] POST + valid session → forward to child (stdin writes serialized via mutex)
- [ ] GET + valid session → SSE notification stream (one per session, 409 if already open)
- [ ] DELETE + valid session → transition to Closing, drain in-flight, kill child (idempotent)
- [ ] Accept header validation: both application/json AND text/event-stream required → 406
- [ ] Content-Type validation: application/json required → 415
- [ ] Invalid/missing session → 404
- [ ] Session closing → 503
- [ ] Session timeout via US-006, respecting active-request counter
- [ ] Health, CORS, headers via US-007/008
- [ ] Backpressure: bounded channel per session
- [ ] Protocol version passthrough: client protocolVersion forwarded exactly to child
- [ ] Notifications from client: forward to child, respond HTTP 202 Accepted
- [ ] Request POSTs: respond with SSE stream (text/event-stream)
- [ ] Server-initiated messages: forward via GET SSE stream
- [ ] Concurrent POSTs: serialized stdin writes, response correlation by JSON-RPC id
- [ ] CORS exposedHeaders: includes Mcp-Session-Id
- [ ] Response lifecycle: session counter inc on accept, dec on finish/close
- [ ] Transport onclose/onerror: clear counter, delete transport, kill child
- [ ] Compatibility harness passes

## Quality Gates
- `cargo +nightly build --release` — no warnings
- `cargo +nightly test` — all tests pass
- `cargo +nightly clippy -- -D warnings` — no warnings
- Compatibility harness passes identically for TS and Rust
notes:
CRITICAL DETAILS FROM PRD:

Supported Protocol Versions: ["2025-06-18", "2025-03-26", "2024-11-05", "2024-10-07"]

Error Response Body Format:
- Transport-level errors (406, 415, 404, 409): plain text body
- Application-level errors (400, 503): JSON-RPC error object {jsonrpc:"2.0", error:{code, message}, id:null}

Body Parsing: Parse JSON body before handler (express.json() equivalent). Unlike SSE mode which reads raw body.

Concurrent POST Response Correlation:
- Multiple POSTs may be in-flight for same session
- Each POST with id gets its own SSE stream response
- Must correlate child stdout responses to the correct POST's SSE stream by matching JSON-RPC id
- Notifications from child go to GET SSE stream (not POST response streams)

title: US-011: Gateway — stdio->Streamable HTTP (Stateless)
description:
Stateless Streamable HTTP gateway: per-request child process with auto-initialization state machine. Each POST spawns a new child, auto-inits if needed, forwards the request, returns response, kills child.

## Tech Stack
- Asupersync web framework for HTTP routing
- Uses: ChildBridge (US-005), CORS (US-007), Logging (US-008), Codec (US-004), AutoInit (mcp_init.rs)

## File: src/gateway/stdio_to_stateless_http.rs, src/mcp_init.rs

## Auto-Init State Machine (mcp_init.rs)
1. Check if incoming request is `initialize` — if yes, forward directly, set isInitialized = true
2. If not initialized and not already auto-initializing:
   a. Store incoming message as pendingOriginalMessage (Vec for batch — fixes TS single-var overwrite bug)
   b. Set isAutoInitializing = true
   c. Generate auto-init request with `--protocolVersion` and synthetic client info
   d. Send initialize request to child stdin
   e. Wait for child response with matching init request ID
   f. Suppress the init response from reaching the client
   g. Send notifications/initialized notification to child
   h. Set isInitialized = true, isAutoInitializing = false
   i. Forward the original pending message(s) to child
3. If already initialized: forward directly

## Auto-Init Request Shape
```json
{
  "jsonrpc": "2.0",
  "id": "init_<timestamp>_<random9>",
  "method": "initialize",
  "params": {
    "protocolVersion": "<--protocolVersion flag value, default 2024-11-05>",
    "capabilities": { "roots": { "listChanged": true }, "sampling": {} },
    "clientInfo": { "name": "supergateway", "version": "<CARGO_PKG_VERSION>" }
  }
}
```

## Request Validation
Same as stateful mode:
1. Accept header: MUST include BOTH application/json AND text/event-stream → 406
2. Content-Type: MUST be application/json → 415
3. GET/DELETE → 405 Method Not Allowed (not 400)

## POST Response Types
Same as stateful: notification-only → 202, request → SSE stream

## Error Handling
- Outer try/catch: unlike stateful, stateless wraps entire handler, returning 500 + JSON-RPC -32603
- Auto-init timeout: 5s → kill child + JSON-RPC -32603
- Child notifications before init response: buffer and forward after init completes
- Child exit during auto-init: Region cancels, return -32603

## Differences from Stateful
- No Mcp-Session-Id header
- No CORS exposedHeaders for Mcp-Session-Id
- No sessionIdGenerator (transport created with undefined equivalent)
- No GET/DELETE endpoints (405)
- Per-request child lifecycle (not per-session)
- Batch pending messages buffered (Vec, not single var — fixes TS bug D-005)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Per-request Region: new child + transport per POST
- [ ] Auto-init state machine per architecture diagram (5 state variables)
- [ ] `--protocolVersion` controls auto-init version string (default: 2024-11-05)
- [ ] Auto-init timeout: 5s, then kill + JSON-RPC -32603
- [ ] Child notifications before init response: buffer and forward after init completes
- [ ] Accept header validation: both application/json AND text/event-stream → 406
- [ ] Content-Type validation: application/json → 415
- [ ] GET/DELETE → 405 Method Not Allowed
- [ ] Notifications from client: forward to child, respond HTTP 202 Accepted
- [ ] Request POSTs: respond with SSE stream
- [ ] Outer try/catch: return 500 + -32603 on unexpected errors
- [ ] No CORS exposedHeaders for Mcp-Session-Id
- [ ] No sessionIdGenerator
- [ ] Batch pending messages: buffer ALL messages (not just one — fixes TS bug)
- [ ] Compatibility harness passes

## Quality Gates
- `cargo +nightly build --release` — no warnings
- `cargo +nightly test` — all tests pass
- `cargo +nightly clippy -- -D warnings` — no warnings
- Compatibility harness passes identically for TS and Rust

title: US-012: Gateway — stdio->SSE
description:
SSE server gateway: single shared child process, broadcast all responses to all connected SSE clients. Implements MCP SSE wire protocol from scratch.

## Tech Stack
- Asupersync web framework for HTTP routing + SSE streaming
- Uses: ChildBridge (US-005), CORS (US-007), Logging (US-008), Codec (US-004)

## File: src/gateway/stdio_to_sse.rs

## SSE Wire Protocol (reimplemented from SDK)

**SSE Connection (GET <ssePath>):**
Response headers (flush immediately, no buffering):
```
HTTP/1.1 200 OK
Content-Type: text/event-stream
Cache-Control: no-cache
Connection: keep-alive
```
Consider adding `X-Accel-Buffering: no` for nginx proxy compatibility.

First event (exactly once per connection):
```
event: endpoint
data: <baseUrl><messagePath>?sessionId=<uuid>

```
- Payload is plain-text URL (NOT JSON)
- URL constructed from `--baseUrl` + `--messagePath` + `?sessionId=<uuid>`
- Without baseUrl, use listener address

Subsequent events:
```
event: message
data: {"jsonrpc":"2.0",...}

```
- Compact single-line JSON (no pretty-printing)
- Each event separated by blank line (\n\n)

**Message POST (POST <messagePath>):**
- Read raw body directly, parse JSON manually (NOT via body-parsing middleware — SDK uses raw-body with 4MB limit)
- Body size limit: 4MB (match SDK default)
- sessionId from query parameter `?sessionId=<uuid>`
- Always returns 202 Accepted for valid POSTs
- Missing sessionId → 400
- No active session → 503

## Broadcast Pattern
Child stdout broadcast to ALL SSE clients:
1. Acquire read lock on client map
2. Clone all client channel senders into local Vec
3. Drop the lock
4. Iterate Vec and send to each (try_send or per-client forwarder task)
This prevents slow clients from blocking others or deadlocking RwLock.

## Backpressure
Per-client bounded channel (256 msgs). Blocked >30s → disconnect client.

## Child Exit
Child exit → gateway process exit with child's exit code (or 1).

## Signal Handling
Explicitly kill child on signal (fixes TS bug where SSE mode omits cleanup callback to onSignals).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Single child spawned at startup
- [ ] GET <ssePath> → SSE connection, endpoint event with POST URL from baseUrl + messagePath
- [ ] POST <messagePath>?sessionId= → forward to child stdin, respond 202 Accepted always
- [ ] --baseUrl support: POST URL in endpoint event = ${baseUrl}${messagePath}?sessionId=${sessionId}
- [ ] Configurable paths: use --ssePath and --messagePath CLI flags
- [ ] Missing sessionId → 400
- [ ] No active session → 503
- [ ] Child stdout broadcast to ALL SSE clients (intentional)
- [ ] Sessions tracked, cleaned on disconnect
- [ ] Dual disconnect detection: both request close AND transport close trigger cleanup
- [ ] Backpressure: per-client bounded channel, blocked >30s → disconnect
- [ ] Rapid connect/disconnect: no leaked tasks, fds, or stale entries after 100+ cycles
- [ ] Custom headers on all responses (SSE stream, POST, health)
- [ ] Body parser skip: read raw body directly, parse JSON manually (4MB limit)
- [ ] Child exit → gateway process exit with child's exit code (or 1)
- [ ] Explicit child kill on signal (fixes TS bug)
- [ ] Compatibility harness passes

## Quality Gates
- `cargo +nightly build --release` — no warnings
- `cargo +nightly test` — all tests pass
- `cargo +nightly clippy -- -D warnings` — no warnings
- Compatibility harness passes identically for TS and Rust

title: US-013: Gateway — stdio->WebSocket
description:
WebSocket gateway: single shared child, per-client ID routing via HashMap multiplexer. Fixes TS string-concatenation ID bugs.

## Tech Stack
- Asupersync WebSocket support (or manual HTTP upgrade + frame handling per US-000 findings)
- Uses: ChildBridge (US-005), CORS (US-007), Logging (US-008), Codec (US-004)

## File: src/gateway/stdio_to_ws.rs

## ID Multiplexer (Fixes TS Bug D-001)
TS version uses string concatenation (`clientId + ':' + msg.id`) which breaks:
- parseInt destroys non-numeric JSON-RPC IDs (string IDs become NaN)
- IDs containing `:` are split incorrectly
- Mutates message object in-place

Rust version: `HashMap<MangledId, (ClientId, OriginalId)>` with auto-increment u64 mangled IDs.

**Message Flow:**
1. Client→Child: Client sends JSON-RPC over WS. Gateway assigns mangled ID (u64), stores (client_id, original_id), rewrites id, forwards to child stdin
2. Child→Client (responses): Child responds with mangled ID. Gateway looks up correlation, restores original ID, sends to correct client WS. Removes entry from map
3. Child→All (notifications): Messages without `id` are broadcast to ALL connected WS clients
4. Stale cleanup: On client disconnect, remove all correlation entries for that client

## Health Endpoint
- HTTP health endpoints with readiness gating (503 before child started + transport ready)
- Fixes TS bug D-002: proper if/else with returns (TS has missing return statements causing "headers already sent")

## --header Support
Custom headers work in WS mode (fixes TS bug D-105 where --header is not passed to WS mode)

## Child Exit
Child exit → gateway process exit.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Single child spawned at startup
- [ ] WebSocket upgrade on <messagePath> (configurable via CLI flag)
- [ ] Proper ID multiplexer: HashMap<MangledId, (ClientId, OriginalId)> — NOT string concatenation
- [ ] Supports string IDs and IDs containing `:` (intentional divergence)
- [ ] Notifications (no id) broadcast to all
- [ ] Responses routed to specific client by JSON-RPC id correlation
- [ ] Stale ID cleanup: remove correlation entries on client disconnect and on response delivery
- [ ] Health endpoints on HTTP with readiness gating (503 before ready)
- [ ] Child exit → gateway process exit
- [ ] Rapid connect/disconnect: no leaked tasks, routing entries, fds
- [ ] --header works in WS mode (intentional improvement over TS)
- [ ] Compatibility harness passes (with divergence flag for ID handling)

## Quality Gates
- `cargo +nightly build --release` — no warnings
- `cargo +nightly test` — all tests pass
- `cargo +nightly clippy -- -D warnings` — no warnings
- Compatibility harness passes identically for TS and Rust
notes:
CRITICAL: Broadcast Without Head-of-Line Blocking pattern:
1. Acquire read lock on client map
2. Clone all client channel senders into a local Vec
3. Drop the lock
4. Iterate Vec and send to each (try_send or per-client spawned forwarder)
NEVER hold RwLock guard across async sends — causes deadlocks.

Backpressure: Per-client bounded channel (256 msgs). Blocked >30s → disconnect client.

Stdin write serialization: Multiple WS clients may send concurrently.
All writes to shared child stdin MUST go through a Mutex to prevent interleaved JSON lines.

title: US-014: HTTP Client Stack
description:
HTTP/1.1 client for making requests to remote MCP servers. Used by both client-mode gateways (SSE→stdio, HTTP→stdio).

## Tech Stack
- Asupersync HTTP client (asupersync::http) or raw TCP with manual HTTP/1.1
- Per US-000 findings for available APIs

## File: src/client/http.rs

## Features
- GET with streaming response (for SSE client)
- POST with JSON body
- Custom headers (per-request) including Mcp-Session-Id management
- Connection keep-alive with bounded pool (max 4 idle per host)

## Timeout Matrix
- Connect: 10s
- Request: 30s
- Idle: 60s

## Safety Rules
- No automatic retry of non-idempotent JSON-RPC POSTs
- Redirects: follow up to 5 redirects for GET, do NOT follow for POST

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] HTTP/1.1 client on asupersync::http or raw TCP
- [ ] GET with streaming response (for SSE client)
- [ ] POST with JSON body
- [ ] Custom headers (per-request)
- [ ] Mcp-Session-Id header management
- [ ] Connection keep-alive with bounded pool (max 4 idle per host)
- [ ] Timeout matrix: connect 10s, request 30s, idle 60s
- [ ] No automatic retry of non-idempotent JSON-RPC POSTs
- [ ] Redirects: follow up to 5 for GET, do NOT follow for POST
- [ ] Unit test against mock server

## Quality Gates
- `cargo +nightly build --release` — no warnings
- `cargo +nightly test` — all tests pass
- `cargo +nightly clippy -- -D warnings` — no warnings

title: US-015: SSE Client (EventSource)
description:
SSE client (EventSource) for connecting to remote SSE MCP servers. Built on top of US-014 HTTP client.

## Tech Stack
- Built on US-014 HTTP client for GET streaming
- Manual text/event-stream parsing

## File: src/client/sse.rs

## SSE Event Parsing
Parse `text/event-stream` format:
- `data:` field — event payload
- `event:` field — event type (default: "message")
- `id:` field — last event ID for reconnection
- `retry:` field — reconnection interval in ms
- Lines starting with `:` are comments (ignore)
- Events separated by blank lines

## Reconnection
- Exponential backoff: 1s, 2s, 4s, ... max 30s
- Include `Last-Event-ID` header on reconnect
- Auth/custom headers preserved on reconnect
- During disconnect: buffer stdin input up to 256 messages, reject with error if buffer full

## Custom Headers
- SSE→stdio uses a custom fetch wrapper to inject headers into EventSource connection
- eventSourceInit.fetch wraps the SSE GET with --header and --oauth2Bearer headers
- This is needed because EventSource doesn't natively support custom headers

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Built on US-014 HTTP client
- [ ] Parse text/event-stream: data/event/id/retry fields
- [ ] Reconnection: exponential backoff (1s, 2s, 4s, ... max 30s) with Last-Event-ID header
- [ ] During disconnect: buffer stdin input up to 256 messages, reject with error if buffer full
- [ ] Auth/custom headers preserved on reconnect
- [ ] Unit test: parse multi-event stream from mock, reconnect simulation

## Quality Gates
- `cargo +nightly build --release` — no warnings
- `cargo +nightly test` — all tests pass
- `cargo +nightly clippy -- -D warnings` — no warnings

title: US-016: Gateway — SSE->stdio
description:
SSE client-mode gateway: bridge stdin JSON-RPC to a remote SSE MCP server. Read stdin, POST to remote; receive via SSE, write to stdout.

## Tech Stack
- Uses: SSE Client (US-015), HTTP Client (US-014), Codec (US-004), JSON-RPC (US-003)
- Singleton client pattern: Arc<Mutex<Option<Client>>>

## File: src/gateway/sse_to_stdio.rs

## Init Dance
1. First stdin message is `initialize`: create MCP client with caller's clientInfo and capabilities
2. First stdin message is NOT `initialize`: create fallback client with default info, connect immediately
3. Protocol version passthrough: intercept initialize request to remote, replace protocolVersion (clean interceptor pattern, not monkey-patch)

## SSE Connection Protocol
1. Client opens GET to SSE URL with custom headers (via eventSourceInit.fetch wrapper)
2. Wait for `event: endpoint` — extract POST URL from data field
3. Subsequent request() calls POST JSON-RPC to discovered endpoint URL
4. If endpoint event never arrives: transport error after timeout
5. requestInit.headers applied to POST requests; eventSourceInit.fetch wraps SSE GET

## Message Forwarding
- Request messages (with method and id): route through MCP client, await response, write to stdout
- All other inbound messages (notifications, server-initiated requests): forward directly to stdout in arrival order without interpretation
- Gateway MUST NOT assume every inbound message corresponds to a locally outstanding request
- Pass-through validation: use permissive/opaque schema (equivalent of z.any())

## Error Handling
- Extract error code and message from caught errors
- Strip SDK-generated prefix `MCP error <code>:` before forwarding error message to stdout
- Wrap response with jsonrpc and id fields
- TS bug fix: fallback path (non-init first message) handles undefined result correctly (TS crashes with TypeError)

## Lifecycle
- Remote transport close → process::exit(1) (triggers pm2/systemd restart)
- Remote transport error → log only, do NOT exit
- Signal handling + stdin EOF/close → graceful shutdown

## Log Direction
Use correct direction labels (TS has reversed labels: 'SSE → Stdio:' for messages going FROM stdio)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Read stdin JSON-RPC, POST to remote SSE server
- [ ] Receive via SSE, write to stdout
- [ ] Init dance: first initialize → client with caller's info; non-init → fallback client
- [ ] Protocol version passthrough (clean interceptor)
- [ ] Custom headers on SSE + POST
- [ ] Error prefix stripping: remove `MCP error <code>:` prefix
- [ ] Non-request message forwarding: all inbound messages forwarded to stdout in order
- [ ] Server-initiated requests: forwarded without interpretation
- [ ] Error responses → JSON-RPC errors on stdout with correct code/message
- [ ] Remote close → process::exit(1). Remote error → log only
- [ ] stdin EOF → graceful shutdown
- [ ] Compatibility harness passes

## Quality Gates
- `cargo +nightly build --release` — no warnings
- `cargo +nightly test` — all tests pass
- `cargo +nightly clippy -- -D warnings` — no warnings
- Compatibility harness passes identically for TS and Rust

title: US-017: Gateway — Streamable HTTP->stdio
description:
Streamable HTTP client-mode gateway: bridge stdin JSON-RPC to a remote Streamable HTTP MCP server.

## Tech Stack
- Uses: HTTP Client (US-014), Codec (US-004), JSON-RPC (US-003)
- Singleton client pattern: Arc<Mutex<Option<Client>>>

## File: src/gateway/http_to_stdio.rs

## Init Dance
Same as US-016 (SSE→stdio):
1. First stdin message is `initialize`: create MCP client with caller's clientInfo and capabilities
2. First stdin message is NOT `initialize`: create fallback client with default info
3. Protocol version passthrough via clean interceptor

## Session Header Management
- Client captures Mcp-Session-Id from server's response headers after initialization
- Client includes Mcp-Session-Id on all subsequent requests
- Client detects response type by Content-Type: application/json (single) vs text/event-stream (streamed)

## Message Forwarding
Same rules as US-016:
- Request messages: route through client, await response, write to stdout
- All other inbound messages: forward directly to stdout in arrival order
- Gateway MUST NOT assume every inbound message corresponds to a locally outstanding request

## Error Handling
Same as US-016:
- Strip `MCP error <code>:` prefix before forwarding
- Fallback path handles undefined result correctly (fixes TS bug)

## Lifecycle
- Remote close → process::exit(1)
- Remote error → log only, do NOT exit
- stdin EOF → graceful shutdown

## Differences from SSE→stdio (US-016)
- No EventSource/SSE for receiving — uses HTTP POST/response
- Mcp-Session-Id header management instead of query parameter sessionId
- Does NOT use custom fetch for EventSource — only uses requestInit.headers for POST requests

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Read stdin, POST to remote Streamable HTTP URL
- [ ] Mcp-Session-Id management (capture from init response, include on subsequent)
- [ ] Same init dance as US-016
- [ ] Custom headers
- [ ] Error prefix stripping: same as US-016
- [ ] Non-request message forwarding: same as US-016
- [ ] Remote close → process::exit(1). Remote error → log only
- [ ] stdin EOF → graceful shutdown
- [ ] Compatibility harness passes

## Quality Gates
- `cargo +nightly build --release` — no warnings
- `cargo +nightly test` — all tests pass
- `cargo +nightly clippy -- -D warnings` — no warnings
- Compatibility harness passes identically for TS and Rust
notes:
CRITICAL: Streamable HTTP response type detection:
- Check Content-Type header: "text/event-stream" → parse as SSE stream (multiple event: message\ndata: <json>\n\n)
- Check Content-Type header: "application/json" → parse as single JSON-RPC response
- SSE stream is the NORMAL case for request POSTs (requests with id)
- Single JSON response is only for some edge cases
Without this, agent will treat all responses as plain JSON and fail.

title: US-018: Benchmark Suite

title: US-003a: GatewayError Type and HTTP Response Mapping
description:
Shared error type hierarchy used by all gateways. Implements Asupersync IntoResponse trait to convert errors to correct HTTP status + JSON-RPC error body.

## File: src/error.rs (new file)

## Error Type
```rust
#[derive(Debug, thiserror::Error)]
enum GatewayError {
    #[error("JSON-RPC error: {code} {message}")]
    JsonRpc { code: i32, message: String },
    #[error("child process error: {0}")]
    Child(#[from] ChildError),
    #[error("session error: {0}")]
    Session(SessionError),
    #[error("transport error: {0}")]
    Transport(String),
    #[error("I/O error: {0}")]
    Io(#[from] std::io::Error),
}
```

## Error Mapping Table (from PRD)
| Condition | HTTP Status | JSON-RPC Error |
|---|---|---|
| Malformed JSON body | 400 | — |
| Valid JSON, invalid JSON-RPC | 200 | -32600 Invalid Request |
| Missing Accept headers (StreamableHTTP) | 406 | — |
| Wrong Content-Type (StreamableHTTP) | 415 | — |
| No session / invalid session (stateful POST) | 404 | — |
| Session closing/closed | 503 | -32000 |
| Child process dead | 502 | -32603 Internal Error |
| Auto-init timeout | 502 | -32603 Internal Error |
| Internal error (stateless catch-all) | 500 | -32603 Internal Error |
| Message too large | 413 | -32600 Invalid Request |
| Max sessions reached | 503 | — |
| GET/DELETE on stateless | 405 | -32000 Method Not Allowed |
| GET SSE stream already open | 409 | — |
| Not ready (health) | 503 | — |
| CORS preflight | 204 | — |
| Notification-only POST | 202 | — |

## IntoResponse Implementation
GatewayError must implement Asupersync's web framework IntoResponse trait. For errors with JSON-RPC codes, response body is:
```json
{"jsonrpc":"2.0","error":{"code":<code>,"message":"<message>"},"id":null}
```
Content-Type: application/json for JSON-RPC error bodies. Plain text for pure HTTP errors (406, 415, etc).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] GatewayError enum with all variants from the error mapping table
- [ ] IntoResponse trait implementation mapping each variant to correct HTTP status
- [ ] JSON-RPC error response body for variants that include JSON-RPC codes
- [ ] Helper constructors: `GatewayError::invalid_session()`, `GatewayError::child_dead()`, etc.
- [ ] Unit tests: each error variant → correct HTTP status + body

## Quality Gates
- `cargo +nightly build --release` — no warnings
- `cargo +nightly test` — all tests pass
- `cargo +nightly clippy -- -D warnings` — no warnings

title: US-002: Project Scaffolding and CLI
description:
Create the Rust project structure with Cargo.toml, toolchain pin, and complete CLI argument parsing using clap derive macros.

## Tech Stack
- Rust nightly (pinned in rust-toolchain.toml, e.g. nightly-2026-03-01)
- `asupersync = "=0.2.7"` (exact pin)
- `clap` (derive) for CLI parsing
- Dependencies: asupersync, clap, serde, serde_json, uuid, nix, regex

## File Structure
```
Cargo.toml
rust-toolchain.toml
src/
  main.rs
  cli.rs
```

## CLI Flags (Complete)
| Flag | Type | Default | Scope |
|------|------|---------|-------|
| `--stdio <cmd>` | string | — | Input |
| `--sse <url>` | string | — | Input |
| `--streamableHttp <url>` | string | — | Input |
| `--outputTransport` | choice | mode-dependent | Output: stdio|sse|ws|streamableHttp |
| `--port` | number | 8000 | Server modes |
| `--baseUrl` | string | '' | stdio->SSE |
| `--ssePath` | string | /sse | stdio->SSE |
| `--messagePath` | string | /message | stdio->SSE, stdio->WS |
| `--streamableHttpPath` | string | /mcp | stdio->StreamableHTTP |
| `--logLevel` | choice | info | All: debug|info|none |
| `--cors [origins]` | array | — | Server modes |
| `--healthEndpoint [eps]` | array | [] | Server modes |
| `--header [headers]` | array | [] | All |
| `--oauth2Bearer <token>` | string | — | All |
| `--stateful` | boolean | false | stdio->StreamableHTTP |
| `--sessionTimeout <ms>` | number | — | Stateful HTTP only |
| `--protocolVersion` | string | 2024-11-05 | Stateless HTTP only |

## Default outputTransport Logic
- If `--sse` or `--streamableHttp` input: default to `stdio`
- If `--stdio` input: default to `sse`
- Use clap-parsed input mode to determine (cleaner than TS raw argv scan)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Cargo.toml with all dependencies, `rust-toolchain.toml` (pinned nightly)
- [ ] clap struct with ALL flags per CLI Flags table. Mode-dependent defaults for `--outputTransport`
- [ ] Mutual exclusivity: exactly one of `--stdio`/`--sse`/`--streamableHttp`
- [ ] Path flags validated to start with `/`
- [ ] `--sessionTimeout` validated positive
- [ ] `--help` output matches TypeScript behavior
- [ ] Exit codes: 0 success, 1 error (match TS)
- [ ] `cargo +nightly build --release` succeeds

## Quality Gates

- `cargo +nightly test` — all tests pass
- `cargo +nightly clippy -- -D warnings` — no warnings

--help criterion clarification: Lists all flags from CLI Flags table with matching camelCase flag names
and mode-dependent defaults documented in help text. Does NOT need character-for-character match with TS output.

Also create DIVERGENCES.md in repo root if it doesn't already exist (it does exist at /c/work/supergateway-rs/DIVERGENCES.md).

title: US-003: JSON-RPC 2.0 Types
description:
Implement JSON-RPC 2.0 message types with serde_json::RawValue for opaque pass-through. This is the foundational protocol layer used by all gateways.

## Tech Stack
- serde + serde_json with RawValue
- IMPORTANT: serde_json::RawValue is a DST (like str). MUST use Box<RawValue> for owned values

## File: src/jsonrpc.rs

## Core Types
```rust
struct RawMessage {
    jsonrpc: String,                        // always "2.0"
    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
    id: Option<Box<RawValue>>,              // number, string, or null — preserved exactly
    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
    method: Option<String>,                 // present for requests/notifications
    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
    params: Option<Box<RawValue>>,          // opaque
    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
    result: Option<Box<RawValue>>,          // opaque
    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
    error: Option<Box<RawValue>>,           // opaque (contains code, message, data)
}

// Helpers
fn is_request(&self) -> bool       // has id + method
fn is_response(&self) -> bool      // has id + (result or error), no method
fn is_notification(&self) -> bool  // has method, no id
fn is_initialize_request(&self) -> bool // method == "initialize"

// Batch: Vec<RawMessage> when input is JSON array
```

## Protocol Transparency Rules
- Unknown methods: forward without interpretation
- Arbitrary params/result shapes: forward as-is
- Arbitrary error.data: forward as-is
- Batch JSON-RPC requests (JSON arrays): forward to child as single line
- JSON-RPC notifications (no id): forward without awaiting response
- Server-initiated requests: forward from child/remote to client without interpretation

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `JsonRpcMessage` enum: Request, Response, Notification, Error
- [ ] `is_initialize_request()` helper
- [ ] `RawMessage` using `serde_json::RawValue` (Box<RawValue>) for opaque pass-through of params/result/error.data
- [ ] Batch support: deserialize JSON array as `Vec<JsonRpcMessage>`
- [ ] Unknown methods: deserialize without rejection
- [ ] Round-trip tests: request, response, notification, error, batch, null id, string id, numeric id, unknown method, large params

## Quality Gates
- `cargo +nightly build --release` — no warnings
- `cargo +nightly test` — all tests pass
- `cargo +nightly clippy -- -D warnings` — no warnings

title: US-004: Line-Delimited Stdio Codec
description:
Async line-delimited codec for reading/writing JSON-RPC messages over stdio pipes. Handles partial UTF-8, buffer overflow, and EOF edge cases.

## Tech Stack
- Async reader from Asupersync (or std with async adapter per US-000 findings)
- Uses RawMessage from US-003 (jsonrpc.rs)
- criterion for benchmarks

## File: src/codec.rs

## Wire Format
- UTF-8 text stream, one JSON document per line
- Line separator: \n (also accept \r\n)
- Compact single-line JSON serialization (no pretty-printing)
- No Content-Length or other framing headers

## Read Behavior
- Buffer incoming data, split on newline
- Partial chunks buffered until newline received
- Blank/whitespace-only lines: skip silently
- Malformed JSON lines: log error, skip, continue reading (not fatal)
- Valid JSON on partial buffer at EOF: emit as final frame

## Write Behavior
- `JSON.stringify(msg) + '\n'` — compact JSON followed by newline
- Writes must be serialized (mutex) for shared-child modes

## Error Handling
- **Invalid UTF-8 from stdout**: fatal protocol violation. Close session, return -32603 to in-flight requests. Log decode error with counter increment
- **Invalid UTF-8 from stderr**: lossy decode (U+FFFD replacement), log, do not crash
- **Max partial buffer size**: 64 MB. Kill child/session on overflow (protocol violation)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `LineCodec` reads async reader, buffers, splits \n, yields `JsonRpcMessage`
- [ ] Handles partial UTF-8 at chunk boundaries correctly
- [ ] Invalid UTF-8 from stdout: fatal protocol violation, close session, return -32603
- [ ] Invalid UTF-8 from stderr: lossy decode (U+FFFD), log, do not crash
- [ ] Skips empty lines, logs+skips invalid JSON (with `decode_errors` counter)
- [ ] `write_message()` serializes + \n, mutex-protected for shared-child modes
- [ ] Max partial buffer size: 64 MB. Kill child/session on overflow
- [ ] EOF handling: trailing buffer valid JSON → emit as final frame. Invalid → log warning, discard
- [ ] Unit tests: partial buffer, empty lines, invalid JSON, >64KB message, rapid sequential, batch (JSON array on single line), invalid UTF-8, EOF with/without trailing newline, buffer overflow
- [ ] criterion benchmark: parse 10k messages

## Quality Gates
- `cargo +nightly build --release` — no warnings
- `cargo +nightly test` — all tests pass
- `cargo +nightly clippy -- -D warnings` — no warnings
notes:
IMPORTANT: Line separator must accept BOTH 
 AND 
 for Windows compatibility.
Split on 
, then strip trailing  from each line before JSON parsing.

title: US-005: Child Process Manager (ChildBridge)
description:
ChildBridge: spawn child MCP servers via `sh -c`, manage process groups with setsid/killpg, handle stdin/stdout/stderr as separate async tasks.

## Tech Stack
- Use `asupersync::process::Command` if available (per US-000 findings), else `std::process::Command` with async wrapper
- `nix` crate for setsid/killpg/waitpid
- Spawned inside Asupersync Region — Region cancel kills child

## File: src/child.rs

## Spawn Sequence
1. `Command::new("sh").arg("-c").arg(stdio_cmd)` with `pre_exec(|| { setsid(); Ok(()) })`
2. `stdin(Stdio::piped())`, `stdout(Stdio::piped())`, `stderr(Stdio::piped())`
3. `.take()` stdin, stdout, stderr into separate async tasks

## Kill Sequence
1. SIGTERM to process group (`killpg(pgid, SIGTERM)`)
2. Wait up to 5s for child exit
3. Check `waitpid(child_pid, WNOHANG)` before SIGKILL (PID reuse safety)
4. If still alive: SIGKILL to process group
5. Drain remaining stdout/stderr (up to 1MB, then truncate with log warning)
6. Close all FDs

## EPIPE Handling
- On broken stdin pipe: mark child as dead immediately, stop accepting new messages
- Propagate error to caller with transport-appropriate status (HTTP 502, WS close, SSE error event)
- Stateful sessions with dead child are terminally failed (no auto-restart — MCP session state lost)

## Stderr Handling
- Captured and logged at error level (all modes consistently)
- Buffer limit: 64KB per read. Lines >4KB truncated with `[truncated]` suffix
- Never queued — logged inline by stderr reader task
- Invalid UTF-8: lossy decode (replace with U+FFFD), do not crash

## Grandchildren
- `setsid` creates new process group. `killpg` sends signal to entire group
- Handles TS bug where `child.kill()` only kills `sh`, leaving `npm`/`node` orphaned

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Spawn via `sh -c` with `pre_exec(setsid)` for process group
- [ ] Use asupersync process Command if available, else std::process::Command with async wrapper
- [ ] Stdin/stdout `.take()`-ed into separate async tasks
- [ ] `kill()`: SIGTERM to pgid -> 5s wait -> SIGKILL (with PID reuse safety check via waitpid WNOHANG)
- [ ] `Drop` impl sends SIGTERM to pgid (safety net)
- [ ] EPIPE detection: on broken stdin pipe, mark child dead, propagate error to caller
- [ ] Child exit detection: detect via wait status, propagate to owning session/gateway
- [ ] Stderr: read + log at error level, 64KB buffer, 4KB line truncation, lossy UTF-8 decode
- [ ] Spawned inside Region — Region cancel kills child
- [ ] Zombie reaping: when running as PID 1, properly reap child processes (SIGCHLD handler with waitpid(-1, WNOHANG) loop)
- [ ] Integration test: spawn, read message, cancel Region, verify pgid killed within 2s
- [ ] Integration test: write to dead child stdin, verify EPIPE handling
- [ ] Integration test: rapid spawn/kill cycles (100+), verify no leaked pids or fds

## Quality Gates
- `cargo +nightly build --release` — no warnings
- `cargo +nightly test` — all tests pass
- `cargo +nightly clippy -- -D warnings` — no warnings
notes:
IMPORTANT: Global memory semaphore — 64MB max partial buffer per child, 256MB global cap across ALL sessions.
Implement as Arc<Semaphore> passed to each ChildBridge on spawn. New sessions rejected (503) when budget exhausted.
This prevents worst case 64MB × 1024 sessions = 64GB memory exhaustion.

title: US-006: Session Manager
description:
Session manager with state machine (Active→Closing→Closed), access counting, timeout, and RAII guards. Used by stateful Streamable HTTP mode.

## Tech Stack
- Asupersync sync primitives: `Arc<RwLock<HashMap<String, Session<S>>>>`
- Asupersync timers for session timeout
- LabRuntime for deterministic testing with virtual time
- uuid crate for cryptographically random session IDs

## File: src/session.rs

## State Machine
```
+--------+    initialize POST     +---------+    DELETE or timeout    +---------+
| (none) | ---------------------->| Active  | ---------------------->| Closing |
+--------+                        +---------+                        +---------+
                                    |     ^                              |
                                    |     |  new POST/GET                |
                                    |     +----(accepted)                |
                                    |                                    | drain in-flight
                                    | POST/GET during close              | kill child
                                    +----> reject 503                    | cleanup
                                                                         v
                                                                     +---------+
                                                                     | Closed  |
                                                                     +---------+
```

## Access Counting
- inc(id) on request accept, dec(id) on HTTP response terminal state (finish, close, error/abort)
- RAII SessionAccessGuard with Drop impl for cancellation-safe decrement:
```rust
struct SessionAccessGuard { session_id: String, counter: Arc<SessionCounter> }
impl Drop for SessionAccessGuard { fn drop(&mut self) { self.counter.dec(&self.session_id); } }
```
- Count→0: start timeout timer (timer holds Weak ref to session map)
- New request during timeout: cancel timer, reactivate
- Timeout MUST NOT fire while active-request counter > 0

## Memory Budget
- Max 1024 concurrent sessions (configurable). Reject with 503 when full
- Max partial line buffer 64MB per child. Global memory semaphore capping total partial buffer to 256MB across all sessions. New sessions rejected (503) when budget exhausted

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `SessionManager<S>` with `Arc<RwLock<HashMap<String, Session<S>>>>`
- [ ] Session state machine: Active -> Closing -> Closed
- [ ] `inc(id)` / `dec(id)` — reference-counted request tracking
- [ ] dec trigger: called when HTTP response reaches terminal state (finish, close, error/abort)
- [ ] Count→0: start timeout timer (timer holds Weak ref to map)
- [ ] New request during timeout: cancel timer, reactivate
- [ ] Timeout fires: remove session, call cleanup callback. Timeout MUST NOT fire while counter > 0
- [ ] `clear(id, run_cleanup)` for explicit teardown (DELETE)
- [ ] DELETE idempotency: DELETE on Closing/Closed returns success
- [ ] Concurrent POST after DELETE: rejected with 503
- [ ] Max sessions limit (1024, configurable). Reject with 503 when full
- [ ] Session ID entropy: use `uuid::Uuid::new_v4()` — cryptographically random
- [ ] Unit tests with LabRuntime: timeout fire, timeout cancel, concurrent inc/dec, clear during timeout, max sessions, state machine transitions, DELETE idempotency, concurrent POST+DELETE race

## Quality Gates
- `cargo +nightly build --release` — no warnings
- `cargo +nightly test` — all tests pass
- `cargo +nightly clippy -- -D warnings` — no warnings

title: US-007: CORS and Headers
description:
CORS middleware and custom header handling. Applied at the application layer (not SDK), before route handlers.

## Tech Stack
- Asupersync web middleware: `App::builder().middleware(cors)`
- `regex` crate for regex CORS origins

## Files: src/cors.rs (CORS), header parsing in src/cli.rs or src/lib.rs

## CORS Origin Types
1. Exact match: `https://example.com`
2. Regex: `/pattern/` (parsed with regex crate)
3. Wildcard: `*` (allow all — but MUST NOT combine with credentials)

## Preflight (OPTIONS)
- Access-Control-Allow-Origin: matched origin or *
- Access-Control-Allow-Methods: GET, POST, DELETE, OPTIONS
- Access-Control-Allow-Headers: Content-Type, Mcp-Session-Id
- Access-Control-Max-Age: 86400 (configurable)
- Stateful mode: Access-Control-Expose-Headers includes Mcp-Session-Id

## Header Parsing
- Format: `Key: Value` — split at first `:` only (values may contain colons)
- Validate non-empty key and non-empty value (after trimming)
- Later headers with same key overwrite earlier ones (last-wins)
- `--oauth2Bearer <token>` adds `Authorization: Bearer <token>` header
- Only add Authorization header if token is actually provided (non-empty string) — fixes TS bug where "Bearer undefined" was sent

## Header Application
Headers applied to ALL gateway-generated HTTP responses:
- SSE stream responses (GET /sse)
- Message POST responses
- Health/readiness responses
- Streamable HTTP responses (POST/GET/DELETE /mcp)
- Error responses (4xx, 5xx)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Exact match, regex (`/pattern/` via regex crate), wildcard
- [ ] OPTIONS preflight: Allow-Origin, Allow-Methods, Allow-Headers, Access-Control-Max-Age (default 86400)
- [ ] Stateful mode: Expose-Headers includes Mcp-Session-Id
- [ ] Credentials: wildcard origin MUST NOT be combined with credentials. Reject config or reflect specific origin
- [ ] `--header "key: value"` and `--oauth2Bearer` parsing
- [ ] Max header size: 8KB. Reject oversized with 431
- [ ] Headers applied to all response types (SSE, POST, health, error responses)
- [ ] Unit tests: each match type, rejection, header parsing, preflight, credentials edge case

## Quality Gates
- `cargo +nightly build --release` — no warnings
- `cargo +nightly test` — all tests pass
- `cargo +nightly clippy -- -D warnings` — no warnings

title: US-008: Logging and Observability
description:
Logging system with [supergateway] prefix, configurable levels, correct stderr/stdout routing, and observability counters exposed via health endpoint.

## Files: src/observe.rs (counters), src/health.rs (health endpoint), logging setup in main.rs

## Logging Rules
- **Prefix**: All log messages prefixed with `[supergateway]`
- **Routing**: When `--outputTransport` is `stdio`, ALL log output goes to stderr (prevents corrupting JSON-RPC stream on stdout). Otherwise: info→stdout, error→stderr
- **Levels**: `none` = no-op, `info` = standard, `debug` = verbose with full-depth object inspection
- **Debug mode**: Full serialization of message contents. No header values logged at info level (only at debug)
- **No timestamps**: Plain text output (timestamps added by pm2/systemd)

## Startup Messages
```
[supergateway] Starting...
[supergateway]   - outputTransport: <value>
```
Followed by mode-specific parameter logging. Omit TS Supermachine promo line.

## Observability Counters
Logged at info level on change, queryable via health endpoint JSON:
| Counter/Gauge | Description |
|---|---|
| active_sessions | Current session count |
| active_children | Current child process count |
| active_clients | Current SSE/WS client count |
| total_requests | Cumulative requests handled |
| total_spawns | Cumulative child processes spawned |
| spawn_failures | Child spawn errors |
| session_timeouts | Sessions cleaned up by timeout |
| forced_kills | Children killed by SIGKILL |
| client_disconnects | Transport disconnections |
| backpressure_events | Times a channel blocked producer |
| queue_depth_max | High-water mark of any channel |
| decode_errors | Invalid UTF-8 or JSON parse failures |

## Health Endpoint
- `GET <configured-path>` returns `"ok"` (plain text) when ready, 503 when not
- `GET <configured-path>?detail=true` returns JSON with all counters (if `--logLevel debug`)
- Readiness conditions: child started + transport bound + relay loop active
- Before readiness and after fatal failure: HTTP 503

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `LogLevel` enum: debug/info/none
- [ ] stdio output transport → all logs to stderr; otherwise info→stdout, error→stderr
- [ ] `[supergateway]` prefix on all messages
- [ ] Plain text, no timestamps
- [ ] Debug mode: full-depth object serialization
- [ ] Counters (see table): logged on change at info level
- [ ] Health endpoint with readiness gating and detail mode
- [ ] No header values logged at info level (only at debug)

## Quality Gates
- `cargo +nightly build --release` — no warnings
- `cargo +nightly test` — all tests pass
- `cargo +nightly clippy -- -D warnings` — no warnings
notes:
Counter implementation pattern:
```rust
pub struct Metrics {
    pub active_sessions: AtomicU64,
    pub active_children: AtomicU64,
    pub active_clients: AtomicU64,
    pub total_requests: AtomicU64,
    pub total_spawns: AtomicU64,
    pub spawn_failures: AtomicU64,
    pub session_timeouts: AtomicU64,
    pub forced_kills: AtomicU64,
    pub client_disconnects: AtomicU64,
    pub backpressure_events: AtomicU64,
    pub queue_depth_max: AtomicU64,
    pub decode_errors: AtomicU64,
}

impl Metrics {
    pub fn inc(&self, counter: &AtomicU64) { counter.fetch_add(1, Ordering::Relaxed); }
    pub fn dec(&self, gauge: &AtomicU64) { gauge.fetch_sub(1, Ordering::Relaxed); }
    pub fn snapshot(&self) -> MetricsSnapshot { /* read all fields */ }
}
```
Shared as Arc<Metrics> across all gateway tasks. All gateways call these methods.
Health detail endpoint calls snapshot() and serializes to JSON.

Log routing: Use eprintln! for all log output when outputTransport is stdio.
Use println! for info, eprintln! for error otherwise. Wrap in a Logger struct.
